summary.intent: Implement a comprehensive set of new charts for the AI metrics dashboard based on a CHARTS.md specification, replacing existing charts with more detailed, user-friendly visualizations including token toggles, tool usage tracking, Russian-language info tooltips, and prompts-per-checkpoint billing visibility.
summary.outcome: All 21 charts were implemented and verified via Playwright, with Russian info popovers on every card, 3-commit rolling averages for smoothing, tool/skill/subagent usage extracted from JSONL transcripts, and a corrected First-Time-Right Rate metric. Build passes with 159/159 tests.

repo: Checkpoint data is cached in src/data/checkpoints-cache.json at build time; the browser refresh button hits unauthenticated GitHub API (60 req/hour limit) and fails with 403 on private repos — must use GITHUB_TOKEN env var with the CLI script instead.

JSONL transcripts contain full assistant messages with content arrays including tool_use blocks, enabling extraction of tool names, Skill IDs, and subagent counts without any pipeline changes beyond extending extractTranscriptData().
MUI v9 Typography does not accept layout props (display, mb, mt) directly — they must be passed via the sx prop.
filterRenderableCheckpoints in ai-metrics-utils.ts is the right place to normalize/backfill missing fields when loading old cached data that predates new schema fields.
The project uses Chart.js (via ChartJsWrapper) and lightweight-charts (via LightweightChartWrapper) as two separate charting libraries depending on chart type.
BMAD command filtering required a separate chart filtered to bmad* prefix, distinct from the general Slash Command Frequency chart.

code.finding: Bug: referenced `checkpoints.length` instead of `cache.checkpoints.length`, causing 'checkpoints is not defined' runtime error.
isFirstTimeRight() originally only checked human_modified===0 and human_removed===0, producing 100% rate. Must also require human_added===0 for a truly pristine AI-only commit.
parseMetadata uses Omit type that needed updating when new fields (tool_usage, skill_usage, subagent_count) were added to CheckpointMeta.
FTR sparkline must filter to only agent_lines > 0 checkpoints to match the stat denominator — mixing eligible and ineligible checkpoints caused a 10 vs 9 mismatch.

workflow: Playwright MCP was used for visual chart verification — navigate, screenshot, evaluate(window.scrollTo), and snapshot tools were combined to inspect all sections.
When adding new data fields to a cached JSON schema, always backfill defaults at the consumption layer (not just the generation layer) to avoid breaking existing cache files.
Running `GITHUB_TOKEN=<token> npx tsx scripts/fetch-checkpoints.ts` is the correct way to refresh data for private repos, not the browser refresh button.
Pre-existing TypeScript errors in a codebase should be verified via git diff before attributing them to new changes — avoids wasted fix attempts.

friction: GitHub API 403 error during browser refresh — unauthenticated rate limit hit on private repo; required switching to CLI script with token.
fetch-checkpoints.ts had a pre-existing bug (checkpoints vs cache.checkpoints) that only surfaced when the script was actually run.

Old cached JSON silently broke the three new JSONL-derived charts (Tool Usage Mix, Top-N Skills, Subagent Timeline) because undefined fields weren't backfilled — required a separate normalization fix.
FTR metric showed 9/9 (100%) initially because the definition was too lenient — human_added lines were not counted as modifications, requiring a logic fix and description update.
FTR sparkline/stat mismatch (10 points vs '1 of 9') required a separate fix to align sparkline filtering with stat denominator.
open_items: Browser refresh button still uses unauthenticated GitHub API — no token support added to the UI; private repo users must always use the CLI script.
Tool Usage Mix, Top-N Skills, and Subagent Timeline charts show 'No data' on old cached JSON — users must refresh cache to see these charts populated.
Pre-existing MUI v9 Typography TypeScript errors (tokensRaw typing in parseMetadata) were left unresolved as they predated this work.
Checkpoint Cadence chart converted to hours but no consideration given to sub-hour cadence display precision for very frequent commits.
